steps.text: The volume of a right circular cylinder is \(V = \pi r^2 h\). The first cylinder has volume \(V_1 = \pi r^2 h\). The second cylinder must have volume \(V_2 = 392 \cdot \pi r^2 h\), so \(\pi R^2 H = 392 \pi r^2 h\), which simplifies to \(R^2 H = 392 r^2 h\).
Factor 392: \(392 = 8 \times 49 = 8 \times 7^2\). So the ratio must satisfy \(\frac{R^2}{r^2} \cdot \frac{H}{h} = 392\).

Test each option by computing \(\frac{R^2}{r^2} \cdot \frac{H}{h}\): Option A gives \(64 \times 7 = 448\). Option B gives \(64 \times 49 = 3{,}136\). Option C gives \(49 \times 8 = 392\). Option D gives \(2{,}401 \times 8 = 19{,}208\).

• Choice A: \(R = 8r\) and \(H = 7h\) gives \((8)^2(7) = 448 eq 392\). Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(R = 8r\) and \(H = 49h\) gives \((8)^2(49) = 3{,}136 eq 392\). Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(R = 7r\) and \(H = 8h\) gives \((7)^2(8) = 49 imes 8 = 392\). That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: \(R = 49r\) and \(H = 8h\) gives \((49)^2(8) = 19{,}208 eq 392\). Eliminated.
